title: Download Boundary Data
text: default, jpmap_build_data() saves converted GeoPackage files package data directory returned jpmap_data_dir(). exact value different computer, website print live path. need create directory hand. jpmap_build_data() creates needed.
code: library(jpmap)  # Run this locally to see your user-specific data directory. jpmap_data_dir(create = FALSE)

title: Download Boundary Data
text: municipal examples, start one prefecture. downloads smaller official MLIT N03 archive, converts GeoPackage, saves jpmap_data_dir(). generated file named prefecture code: Okinawa, package already includes 2024 file:
code: jpmap_build_data(year = 2024, prefecture = "Ehime") jpmap_boundaries_2024_38.gpkg jpmap_boundaries_2024_47.gpkg

title: Download Boundary Data
text: Build nationwide municipal data need municipalities. official 2024 national source archive 583 MB, converted GeoPackage also large. generated national file named:
code: jpmap_build_data(year = 2024) jpmap_boundaries_2024.gpkg

title: Download Boundary Data
text: source MLIT National Land Numerical Information N03 administrative area data: 2024 data page: https://nlftp.mlit.go.jp/ksj/gml/datalist/KsjTmplt-N03-2024.html national 2024 archive: https://nlftp.mlit.go.jp/ksj/gml/data/N03/N03-2024/N03-20240101_GML.zip Okinawa 2024 archive: https://nlftp.mlit.go.jp/ksj/gml/data/N03/N03-2024/N03-20240101_47_GML.zip filename pattern N03-YYYY0101_GML.zip national data N03-YYYY0101_PP_GML.zip one-prefecture data, PP two-digit prefecture code.

title: Download Boundary Data
text: want keep boundary data project folder instead default user data directory, pass destdir. Use folder later data_dir.
code: dir.create("jpmap-data", showWarnings = FALSE) jpmap_build_data(year = 2024, prefecture = "Ehime", destdir = "jpmap-data") jp_map("municipality", include = "Ehime", data_dir = "jpmap-data")

title: FAQ
text: Japan spans long north-south east-west range. geographically literal map can make smaller islands difficult see. jpmap follows usmap idea using insets important regions remain visible ordinary plots. Use inset = FALSE, inset = "okinawa", inset = "ogasawara" want exclude one transported island groups. can also use okinawa = FALSE ogasawara = FALSE, mirroring style UchidaMizuki/jpmap. plot_jpmap() draws inset boxes default; set inset_boxes = FALSE remove . boxes visual guide frames transported inset clusters sized cover Okinawa Ogasawara source extents jpmap transports. display frames, legal extents. boxes shown, longitude/latitude lines inside local graticules transported island group, show true island coordinates rather destination coordinates box.

title: FAQ
text: package includes -prefecture example boundaries official MLIT N03 municipal boundaries Okinawa Prefecture January 1, 2024. Large nationwide municipal files kept outside package. Use jpmap_build_data() build local GeoPackage MLIT N03 administrative area data. Use jpmap_build_data(prefecture = "Ehime") need one prefecture.

title: FAQ
text: API supports regions = "prefectures" regions = "municipalities".

what: Can I add my own points or lines?
title: FAQ
text: Yes. Use jpmap_transform() data frame, sf object, sfc geometry vector, add plot_jpmap() map ggplot2 layers.

title: Import Boundary Data
text: jp_map() returns sf object. Without extra options, prefecture maps use bundled -Japan prefecture file.
code: prefectures <- jp_map("prefecture") prefectures #> Simple feature collection with 47 features and 4 fields #> Geometry type: MULTIPOLYGON #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: -1301914 ymin: -862391 xmax: 1382212 ymax: 952450.3 #> Projected CRS: +proj=laea +lat_0=37.5 +lon_0=137.5 +x_0=0 +y_0=0 +datum=WGS84 +units=m +no_defs #> First 10 features: #>    jis_code pref_code prefecture prefecture_ja                           geom #> 1        01        01   Hokkaido        北海道 MULTIPOLYGON (((511996.2 75... #> 2        02        02     Aomori        青森県 MULTIPOLYGON (((207443 3278... #> 3        03        03      Iwate        岩手県 MULTIPOLYGON (((354100.1 33... #> 4        04        04     Miyagi        宮城県 MULTIPOLYGON (((358110.3 17... #> 5        05        05      Akita        秋田県 MULTIPOLYGON (((205918 1818... #> 6        06        06   Yamagata        山形県 MULTIPOLYGON (((178613.3 11... #> 7        07        07  Fukushima        福島県 MULTIPOLYGON (((301336.6 48... #> 8        08        08    Ibaraki        茨城県 MULTIPOLYGON (((285086.4 -6... #> 9        09        09    Tochigi        栃木県 MULTIPOLYGON (((175647.2 -1... #> 10       10        10      Gunma        群馬県 MULTIPOLYGON (((140563.2 -5...

title: Import Boundary Data
text: package includes official MLIT N03 municipal boundaries Okinawa Prefecture January 1, 2024. works without data_year data_dir jpmap can see bundled Okinawa file.
code: okinawa_municipalities <- jp_map("municipality", include = "Okinawa") okinawa_municipalities #> Simple feature collection with 5721 features and 7 fields #> Geometry type: GEOMETRY #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: -1302554 ymin: 381372.8 xmax: -228994.3 ymax: 881725 #> Projected CRS: +proj=laea +lat_0=37.5 +lon_0=137.5 +x_0=0 +y_0=0 +datum=WGS84 +units=m +no_defs #> First 10 features: #>    jis_code pref_code prefecture prefecture_ja municipality_code #> 1     47000        47    Okinawa        沖縄県             47000 #> 2     47201        47    Okinawa        沖縄県             47201 #> 3     47201        47    Okinawa        沖縄県             47201 #> 4     47201        47    Okinawa        沖縄県             47201 #> 5     47201        47    Okinawa        沖縄県             47201 #> 6     47201        47    Okinawa        沖縄県             47201 #> 7     47201        47    Okinawa        沖縄県             47201 #> 8     47201        47    Okinawa        沖縄県             47201 #> 9     47205        47    Okinawa        沖縄県             47205 #> 10    47207        47    Okinawa        沖縄県             47207 #>    municipality_ja municipality_full_ja                           geom #> 1       所属未定地           所属未定地 POLYGON ((-660668.8 686316.... #> 2           那覇市               那覇市 POLYGON ((-688261.6 653232.... #> 3           那覇市               那覇市 POLYGON ((-686149.7 660762,... #> 4           那覇市               那覇市 POLYGON ((-687930.7 658623.... #> 5           那覇市               那覇市 POLYGON ((-684618.2 659034.... #> 6           那覇市               那覇市 POLYGON ((-684986.2 659017.... #> 7           那覇市               那覇市 POLYGON ((-684460.9 658946.... #> 8           那覇市               那覇市 POLYGON ((-684330.1 658981.... #> 9         宜野湾市             宜野湾市 POLYGON ((-673274 661798.1,... #> 10          石垣市               石垣市 POLYGON ((-1158043 462743.9...

title: Import Boundary Data
text: building local file, use include value select prefecture. saved file somewhere else, pass folder data_dir.
code: jpmap_build_data(year = 2024, prefecture = "Ehime") ehime_municipalities <- jp_map("municipality", include = "Ehime", data_year = 2024) ehime_municipalities <- jp_map(   "municipality",   include = "Ehime",   data_year = 2024,   data_dir = "jpmap-data" )

title: Import Boundary Data
text: GeoPackage layers ordinary spatial data layers, can inspect sf. Read layer directly need lower-level control. map workflows can use jp_map() instead.
code: okinawa_file <- available$path[available$year == 2024 & available$pref_code == "47"]  sf::st_layers(okinawa_file) #> Driver: GPKG  #> Available layers: #>       layer_name geometry_type features fields crs_name #> 1    prefectures Multi Polygon        1      4   WGS 84 #> 2 municipalities       Polygon     5818      7   WGS 84 municipalities <- sf::st_read(okinawa_file, layer = "municipalities")

title: Import Boundary Data
text: jpmap recognizes file names: jpmap_boundaries_YYYY.gpkg national data; jpmap_boundaries_YYYY_PP.gpkg one-prefecture data. PP suffix two-digit prefecture code, 38 Ehime 47 Okinawa.

title: Okinawa and Ogasawara Insets
text: Use ogasawara = FALSE want Okinawa moved.  Use okinawa = FALSE want Ogasawara moved.
code: plot_jpmap(   "prefecture",   ogasawara = FALSE ) +   labs(title = "Okinawa inset only") +   theme(     plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = "#2C2A29")   ) plot_jpmap(   "prefecture",   okinawa = FALSE ) +   labs(title = "Ogasawara inset only") +   theme(     plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = "#2C2A29")   )

title: Okinawa and Ogasawara Insets
text: Use xlim ylim set longitude/latitude limits. can also set axis breaks labels. example keeps main islands Okinawa dropping Ogasawara inset.
code: plot_jpmap(   "prefecture",   ogasawara = FALSE,   xlim = c(122, 149),   ylim = c(28.5, 47),   x_breaks = seq(125, 145, 5),   y_breaks = seq(30, 45, 5),   x_labels = function(x) paste0(x, "\u00b0E"),   y_labels = function(y) paste0(y, "\u00b0N") ) +   labs(title = "Main islands with Okinawa inset") +   theme(     plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = "#2C2A29")   )

title: Okinawa and Ogasawara Insets
text: Set inset_boxes = FALSE keep transported islands remove visual frames. boxes shown, jpmap masks ordinary main-panel graticules inside box draws local longitude/latitude lines transported island group. labels islands’ original coordinates, destination coordinates inset box.
code: plot_jpmap(   "prefecture",   inset_boxes = FALSE ) +   labs(title = "Insets without boxes") +   theme(     plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = "#2C2A29")   )

title: Okinawa and Ogasawara Insets
text: Set inset = FALSE keep every geometry projected geographic location.
code: plot_jpmap(   "prefecture",   inset = FALSE ) +   labs(title = "Projected map without transported insets") +   theme(     plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = "#2C2A29")       )

title: Okinawa and Ogasawara Insets
text: Areas discussed Japan territorial-dispute references excluded default. Set territorial_disputes = TRUE add cartographic island/reef shapes Northern Territories, Okinotorishima, Senkaku Islands, Takeshima / Liancourt Rocks. can also pass character vector include selected regions, territorial_disputes = "senkaku" territorial_disputes = c("senkaku", "takeshima"). plot_jpmap() gives opt-disputed shapes separate fill dot overlay small islands reefs remain visible default scale. Use disputed_fill, disputed_color, disputed_dots = FALSE change styling.
code: plot_jpmap(   "prefecture",   territorial_disputes = TRUE ) +   labs(title = "Map with opt-in disputed-territory shapes") +   theme(     plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = "#2C2A29")   )

title: Okinawa and Ogasawara Insets
text: inset boxes visual guide frames transported island groups. sized cover Okinawa Ogasawara source extents jpmap transports default map frame. longitude/latitude labels drawn inside box describe island group’s true coordinates transport. boxes still display frames, legal boundary extents. Okinawa municipal maps, bundled 2024 MLIT N03 Okinawa layer transported Okinawa. Ogasawara, default box also covers remote Ogasawara pieces present bundled prefecture layer, Minamitorishima. Use inset = FALSE literal geographic placement matters compact display. territorial_disputes = TRUE includes Okinotorishima, Ogasawara box expanded opt-Okinotorishima reef shape remains visible. disputed-territory shapes official boundary polygons.

title: Transform Data
code: library(jpmap)  places <- data.frame(   place = c("Tokyo", "Naha", "Ogasawara"),   lon = c(139.767, 127.681, 142.191),   lat = c(35.681, 26.212, 27.094) )  jpmap_transform(places, output_names = c("x", "y")) #>       place     lon    lat         x         y #> 1     Tokyo 139.767 35.681  205212.3 -199413.7 #> 2      Naha 127.681 26.212 -683245.1  657516.5 #> 3 Ogasawara 142.191 27.094  537218.7 -578088.9

title: Transform Data
text: data sf object, jpmap_transform() returns sf object. Use inset = FALSE want projected Japan CRS Okinawa/Ogasawara inset movement. can also move one island group. switches can written boolean arguments want default inset = TRUE behavior except one island group.
code: pts <- sf::st_as_sf(   places,   coords = c("lon", "lat"),   crs = 4326,   remove = FALSE )  jpmap_transform(pts) #> Simple feature collection with 3 features and 3 fields #> Geometry type: POINT #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: -683245.1 ymin: -578088.9 xmax: 537218.7 ymax: 657516.5 #> Projected CRS: +proj=laea +lat_0=37.5 +lon_0=137.5 +x_0=0 +y_0=0 +datum=WGS84 +units=m +no_defs #>       place     lon    lat                   geometry #> 1     Tokyo 139.767 35.681 POINT (205212.3 -199413.7) #> 2      Naha 127.681 26.212 POINT (-683245.1 657516.5) #> 3 Ogasawara 142.191 27.094 POINT (537218.7 -578088.9) jpmap_transform(places, output_names = c("x", "y"), inset = FALSE) #>       place     lon    lat         x          y #> 1     Tokyo 139.767 35.681  205212.3  -199413.7 #> 2      Naha 127.681 26.212 -983633.2 -1201884.3 #> 3 Ogasawara 142.191 27.094  466806.8 -1141584.2 jpmap_transform(places, output_names = c("x", "y"), inset = "okinawa") #>       place     lon    lat         x          y #> 1     Tokyo 139.767 35.681  205212.3  -199413.7 #> 2      Naha 127.681 26.212 -683245.1   657516.5 #> 3 Ogasawara 142.191 27.094  466806.8 -1141584.2 jpmap_transform(places, output_names = c("x", "y"), inset = "ogasawara") #>       place     lon    lat         x          y #> 1     Tokyo 139.767 35.681  205212.3  -199413.7 #> 2      Naha 127.681 26.212 -983633.2 -1201884.3 #> 3 Ogasawara 142.191 27.094  537218.7  -578088.9 jpmap_transform(places, output_names = c("x", "y"), okinawa = FALSE) #>       place     lon    lat         x          y #> 1     Tokyo 139.767 35.681  205212.3  -199413.7 #> 2      Naha 127.681 26.212 -983633.2 -1201884.3 #> 3 Ogasawara 142.191 27.094  537218.7  -578088.9 jpmap_transform(places, output_names = c("x", "y"), ogasawara = FALSE) #>       place     lon    lat         x          y #> 1     Tokyo 139.767 35.681  205212.3  -199413.7 #> 2      Naha 127.681 26.212 -683245.1   657516.5 #> 3 Ogasawara 142.191 27.094  466806.8 -1141584.2

title: Authors and Citation

code: @Manual{,   title = {jpmap: Japan Maps with Insets for Okinawa and Ogasawara},   author = {Yusaku Horiuchi},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://yhoriuchi.github.io/jpmap/}, }

title: jpmap
text: Install development version GitHub:
code: install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("yhoriuchi/jpmap")

title: jpmap
code: library(jpmap)  plot_jpmap("prefecture") plot_jpmap("municipality", include = "Okinawa") plot_jpmap("prefecture", territorial_disputes = TRUE)

title: jpmap
text: Start pages: Introduction Download Boundary Data Import Boundary Data Transform Data Okinawa Ogasawara Insets use plotting tutorials: Plot Prefectural Choropleth Maps Plot Prefectural Point Maps Plot Municipal Choropleth Maps Plot Municipal Point Maps

title: jpmap
text: Use jpmap_transform() put user-supplied longitude latitude data projected coordinate system used plot_jpmap().
code: library(jpmap)  points <- data.frame(   place = c("Tokyo", "Naha", "Ogasawara"),   lon = c(139.767, 127.681, 142.191),   lat = c(35.681, 26.212, 27.094) )  jpmap_transform(points)

title: jpmap
text: installed package includes: -prefecture example boundaries Japan, based Natural Earth Admin-1 data; official MLIT N03 municipal boundaries Okinawa Prefecture January 1, 2024. Use bundled Okinawa municipal data immediately: Nationwide municipal polygons much larger built locally Japan’s official MLIT National Land Numerical Information N03 administrative area data: generated file written jpmap_data_dir() default contains two layers: prefectures municipalities data available, jp_map() returns sf objects plot_jpmap() returns ordinary ggplot2 maps. Users already work jpndistrict can also pass sf output jpmap_transform().
code: plot_jpmap("municipality", include = "Okinawa") jpmap_build_data(year = 2024) jpmap_build_data(year = 2024, prefecture = "Ehime")

title: jpmap
text: Two public-source sample datasets included: jp_prefecture_gdp: 2021 prefecture GDP per capita values. jp_us_military_bases: selected U.S. military installations Japan coordinates, public approximate personnel figures available, row-level source_url links.

what: Retrieve Disputed-Territory Island And Reef Shapes — jp_disputed_territories
title: Retrieve Disputed-Territory Island And Reef Shapes — jp_disputed_territories
text: Returns cartographic island/reef shapes disputed territories disputed maritime/EEZ-status areas discussed Japan territorial-dispute references. geometries intentionally separate MLIT N03 administrative boundary data authoritative legal boundary polygons.

title: Retrieve Disputed-Territory Island And Reef Shapes — jp_disputed_territories
code: jp_disputed_territories(   territorial_disputes = TRUE,   regions = c("prefectures", "prefecture", "municipalities", "municipality"),   inset = TRUE,   okinawa = TRUE,   ogasawara = TRUE )

title: Retrieve Disputed-Territory Island And Reef Shapes — jp_disputed_territories
text: territorial_disputes disputed-territory shapes include. Use TRUE built-shapes, FALSE none, character vector containing one "northern_territories", "okinotorishima", "senkaku", "takeshima". Common aliases "kuril", "liancourt", "dokdo", "diaoyu" also accepted. regions Boundary level whose columns mirrored: prefectures municipalities. inset Inset behavior. Use TRUE move Okinawa Ogasawara, FALSE literal projected locations, character vector containing "okinawa" /"ogasawara". okinawa Whether Okinawa moved inset includes . ogasawara Whether Ogasawara moved inset includes .

title: Retrieve Disputed-Territory Island And Reef Shapes — jp_disputed_territories
text: sf data frame.

title: Retrieve Disputed-Territory Island And Reef Shapes — jp_disputed_territories
text: Territory list based https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Territorial_disputes_of_Japan. Shapes derived Natural Earth 1:10m land polygons OpenStreetMap geometry.

title: Retrieve Disputed-Territory Island And Reef Shapes — jp_disputed_territories
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ jp_disputed_territories() jp_disputed_territories(c("senkaku", "takeshima")) } # }

title: Retrieve Japan Map Data — jp_map
text: Reads Japan administrative boundary data returns sf object.

title: Retrieve Japan Map Data — jp_map
code: jp_map(   regions = c("prefectures", "prefecture", "municipalities", "municipality"),   include = c(),   exclude = c(),   data_year = NULL,   inset = TRUE,   okinawa = TRUE,   ogasawara = TRUE,   territorial_disputes = FALSE,   data_dir = NULL )

title: Retrieve Japan Map Data — jp_map
text: regions Boundary level: prefectures municipalities. include Regions include code, English name, Japanese name. exclude Regions exclude code, English name, Japanese name. data_year Boundary data year. newest appropriate available file used default. example, national prefecture maps prefer national file, one-prefecture municipal requests can use matching prefecture file. inset Inset behavior. Use TRUE move Okinawa Ogasawara, FALSE movement, character vector containing "okinawa" /"ogasawara" move selected island groups. okinawa Whether Okinawa moved inset includes . ogasawara Whether Ogasawara moved inset includes . territorial_disputes Whether include disputed-territory island/reef shapes. default FALSE. Use TRUE built-shapes, character vector containing one "northern_territories", "okinotorishima", "senkaku", "takeshima". data_dir Optional directory containing jpmap_boundaries_YYYY.gpkg.

title: Retrieve Japan Map Data — jp_map
text: sf data frame.

title: Retrieve Japan Map Data — jp_map
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ jp_map("prefectures") jp_map("prefectures", okinawa = FALSE) jp_map("prefectures", territorial_disputes = TRUE) jp_map("municipalities", include = "Okinawa") jp_map("prefecture") jp_map("municipality", include = "Okinawa") } # }

what: Join Data to a jpmap Map — jp_map_with_data
title: Join Data to a jpmap Map — jp_map_with_data
text: Joins user data Japan map object.

title: Join Data to a jpmap Map — jp_map_with_data
code: jp_map_with_data(map, data, values = NULL, by = NULL)

title: Join Data to a jpmap Map — jp_map_with_data
text: map sf object returned jp_map(). data data frame containing matching administrative code name column. values Optional value column check joining. Optional join column. omitted, jpmap guesses common columns.

title: Join Data to a jpmap Map — jp_map_with_data
text: sf object.

what: GDP per capita by Japanese prefecture — jp_prefecture_gdp
title: GDP per capita by Japanese prefecture — jp_prefecture_gdp
text: sample data frame containing 2021 prefecture GDP per capita values.

title: GDP per capita by Japanese prefecture — jp_prefecture_gdp
text: data frame 47 rows 6 variables: pref_code Two-digit Japanese prefecture code. prefecture Prefecture name English. year Reference year. gdp_per_capita_jpy GDP per capita Japanese yen. gdp_per_capita_usd_ppp GDP per capita U.S. dollars PPP. source Source note.

title: GDP per capita by Japanese prefecture — jp_prefecture_gdp
text: OECD 2021 regional GDP per-capita values tabulated https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Japanese_prefectures_by_GDP_per_capita.

what: Selected U.S. military installations in Japan — jp_us_military_bases
title: Selected U.S. military installations in Japan — jp_us_military_bases
text: sample data frame containing selected U.S. military installations Japan longitude/latitude coordinates public approximate personnel figures figures clear source.

title: Selected U.S. military installations in Japan — jp_us_military_bases
text: data frame 17 rows 12 variables: base Installation command name. branch Primary U.S. military branch. prefecture Japanese prefecture. municipality Municipality municipalities. lon Longitude WGS84. lat Latitude WGS84. personnel Approximate public personnel figure, available. personnel_scope public figure counts. personnel_geography Whether figure describes installation, regional total, command, broader installation community. personnel_is_base_specific Whether personnel interpreted base-specific figure. source_url Source URL. note Caveat use examples.

title: Selected U.S. military installations in Japan — jp_us_military_bases
text: values intended examples official personnel accounting. rows describe broader installation, regional, command, community populations; see personnel_scope personnel_geography.

title: Selected U.S. military installations in Japan — jp_us_military_bases
text: Public installation, command, regional pages listed source_url column, including https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Misawa_Air_Base, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yokota_Air_Base, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Okinawa_Island.

what: jpmap: Japan Maps with Insets for Okinawa and Ogasawara — jpmap-package
title: jpmap: Japan Maps with Insets for Okinawa and Ogasawara — jpmap-package
text: Tools drawing maps Japan prefecture municipal boundaries. API mirrors main workflow usmap package.

what: Coordinate Reference System for jpmap — jpmap_crs
title: Coordinate Reference System for jpmap — jpmap_crs
text: Returns projected coordinate reference system used jpmap.

title: Coordinate Reference System for jpmap — jpmap_crs
code: jpmap_crs()

title: Coordinate Reference System for jpmap — jpmap_crs
text: sf::st_crs() object.

title: Manage jpmap Boundary Data — jpmap_data
text: Helpers locating building GeoPackage boundary data used jpmap. installed package includes example prefecture boundaries official MLIT N03 municipal boundaries Okinawa Prefecture January 1, 2024. Use jpmap_build_data() build nationwide detailed municipal boundaries, jpmap_build_data(prefecture = "Ehime") build one prefecture official MLIT N03 administrative area data.

title: Manage jpmap Boundary Data — jpmap_data
code: jpmap_data_dir(create = TRUE)  available_jpmap_data(data_dir = NULL)  jpmap_build_data(   year = 2024,   prefecture = NULL,   destdir = jpmap_data_dir(),   url = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE,   simplify_tolerance = NULL )

title: Manage jpmap Boundary Data — jpmap_data
text: create Whether create default data directory. data_dir Optional directory scan boundary data. year Boundary data year. prefecture Optional prefecture code, English name, Japanese name. supplied, prefecture's official MLIT N03 file downloaded built. destdir Directory generated GeoPackage written. url Optional source URL. default, MLIT N03 URL constructed. overwrite Whether overwrite existing GeoPackage. quiet Whether suppress messages download spatial reads/writes. simplify_tolerance Optional tolerance passed sf::st_simplify().

title: Manage jpmap Boundary Data — jpmap_data
text: jpmap_data_dir() returns path, available_jpmap_data() returns data frame year, pref_code, prefecture, path, jpmap_build_data() invisibly returns generated file.

title: Manage jpmap Boundary Data — jpmap_data
text: MLIT National Land Numerical Information N03 administrative area data: https://nlftp.mlit.go.jp/ksj/gml/datalist/KsjTmplt-N03-2024.html.

title: Manage jpmap Boundary Data — jpmap_data
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ jpmap_build_data(year = 2024) jpmap_build_data(year = 2024, prefecture = "Ehime") available_jpmap_data() } # }

what: Transform Data to the jpmap Projection — jpmap_transform
title: Transform Data to the jpmap Projection — jpmap_transform
text: Transforms geographic data jpmap projected coordinate system. inset = TRUE, Okinawa Ogasawara moved visible inset locations. Use okinawa = FALSE, ogasawara = FALSE, inset = "okinawa", inset = "ogasawara" transport one island group.

title: Transform Data to the jpmap Projection — jpmap_transform
code: jpmap_transform(   data,   input_names = c("lon", "lat"),   output_names = input_names,   inset = TRUE,   okinawa = TRUE,   ogasawara = TRUE )

title: Transform Data to the jpmap Projection — jpmap_transform
text: data sf object, sfc geometry vector, data frame. input_names Longitude latitude column names data frames. output_names Output coordinate column names data frames. inset Inset behavior. Use TRUE move Okinawa Ogasawara, FALSE movement, character vector containing "okinawa" /"ogasawara" move selected island groups. okinawa Whether Okinawa moved inset includes . ogasawara Whether Ogasawara moved inset includes .

Plot a Japan Map — plot_jpmap
text: Plots Japan prefecture municipal boundaries using ggplot2.

title: Plot a Japan Map — plot_jpmap
code: plot_jpmap(   regions = c("prefectures", "prefecture", "municipalities", "municipality"),   include = c(),   exclude = c(),   data = data.frame(),   values = NULL,   labels = FALSE,   label_color = "black",   data_year = NULL,   inset = TRUE,   okinawa = TRUE,   ogasawara = TRUE,   territorial_disputes = FALSE,   disputed_fill = "#F6C85F",   disputed_color = "#2C2A29",   disputed_linewidth = 0.35,   disputed_dots = TRUE,   disputed_dot_fill = "#2C2A29",   disputed_dot_color = "white",   disputed_dot_size = 1.25,   disputed_dot_stroke = 0.2,   inset_boxes = TRUE,   inset_box_color = "grey50",   inset_box_linewidth = 0.35,   data_dir = NULL,   xlim = NULL,   ylim = NULL,   x_breaks = ggplot2::waiver(),   y_breaks = ggplot2::waiver(),   x_labels = ggplot2::waiver(),   y_labels = ggplot2::waiver(),   fill = "grey92",   color = "grey35",   linewidth = 0.25,   ... )

title: Plot a Japan Map — plot_jpmap
text: regions Boundary level: prefectures municipalities. include Regions include code, English name, Japanese name. exclude Regions exclude code, English name, Japanese name. data Optional data frame join map. values Column name data use fill variable. labels Whether draw region labels. label_color Label text color. data_year Boundary data year. inset Inset behavior. Use TRUE move Okinawa Ogasawara, FALSE movement, character vector containing "okinawa" /"ogasawara" move selected island groups. okinawa Whether Okinawa moved inset includes . ogasawara Whether Ogasawara moved inset includes . territorial_disputes Whether include disputed-territory island/reef shapes. default FALSE. Use TRUE built-shapes, character vector containing one "northern_territories", "okinotorishima", "senkaku", "takeshima". disputed_fill Fill color opt-disputed-territory shapes. disputed_color Outline color opt-disputed-territory shapes. disputed_linewidth Line width opt-disputed-territory shapes. disputed_dots Whether draw dot markers opt-disputed-territory shapes. disputed_dot_fill Fill color disputed-territory dot markers. disputed_dot_color Outline color disputed-territory dot markers. disputed_dot_size Size disputed-territory dot markers. disputed_dot_stroke Stroke width disputed-territory dot markers. inset_boxes Whether draw boxes around transported Okinawa Ogasawara insets. inset_box_color Outline color inset boxes. inset_box_linewidth Line width inset boxes. data_dir Optional directory containing jpmap_boundaries_YYYY.gpkg. xlim, ylim Optional longitude latitude limits plot frame. x_breaks, y_breaks Optional longitude latitude axis breaks. x_labels, y_labels Optional longitude latitude axis labels. fill Boundary fill color values supplied. color Boundary line color. linewidth Boundary line width. ... Additional arguments passed ggplot2::geom_sf().

title: Plot a Japan Map — plot_jpmap
text: ggplot2 plot.

title: Plot a Japan Map — plot_jpmap
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ plot_jpmap("prefecture") plot_jpmap("prefecture", ogasawara = FALSE) plot_jpmap("prefecture", territorial_disputes = TRUE) plot_jpmap(   "prefecture",   ogasawara = FALSE,   xlim = c(122, 149),   ylim = c(28.5, 47),   x_breaks = seq(125, 145, 5),   y_breaks = seq(30, 45, 5) ) plot_jpmap("prefecture", inset_boxes = FALSE) plot_jpmap("municipality", include = "Okinawa") } # }

title: jpmap 0.0.0.9000
text: Initial development version. Added usmap-style API Japan maps: jp_map(), plot_jpmap(), jpmap_transform(), jpmap_crs(). Added support prefecture municipal boundary layers. Added inset transformations Okinawa Ogasawara, support selecting island groups transport via inset. Added pkgdown website configuration using Florida State University garnet gold colors. Added sample GDP-per-capita U.S. military base datasets plus website vignette showing Japan map. military-base example now separates installation locations regional, command-level, base-specific public personnel figures.
